observations.summary: Aviel accuses the listener of being like his mother, who used to send messages and riddles through dreams, implying a pattern of meddling and interfering behavior.

evidence.quote: "Your mother used to do this," Aviel said, and his voice had gone flat, affectless. "Step into my dreams. Leave her little messages. Her riddles." His hands, which had been reaching for the sack, slowly curled back into fists. "You're just like her. Meddling. Interfering. Unable to leave well enough alone."

observations.notes: This observation highlights a familial and psychological motif of interference and unresolved issues.
